description: Goal: route all activation-key construction through the derived partition policy while keeping ActivationInputs as the joined raw-evidence fact consumed by semantic analysis. Signal: world/semantic tests prove distinct raw caller contributions can share one activation key, ActivationInputs joins and retracts publisher-owned evidence correctly, key coarsening triggers reanalysis only when the joined evidence changes, and closure-call resolution still discovers every reachable callee from evidence rather than from key identity. Strategy: replace canonical_activation_key's recursive/list-only branch with policy-driven projection; keep the key/value split explicit in names and APIs; update seed-root, direct-call, closure-call, protocol-call, and latent callable activation paths to use the same keying rule. Outro: no code path reads ActivationKey input as body evidence; no path applies convergence only because a function is recursive; no caller-owned evidence is lost during key projection.
title: Compiler2 world: canonicalize activation keys through partition policy while preserving input evidence
